skill.name: turborepo-caching
skill.description: Configure Turborepo caching for monorepo builds, CI pipelines, package filters, and local or remote cache workflows.
skill.summary: Set up and optimize Turborepo caching for faster monorepo builds.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis raised many alerts, but most command, URL, and environment-file findings are Markdown examples or Turborepo configuration guidance, not executable skill behavior. No prompt injection or credential exfiltration was found, but the self-hosted remote cache template is unsafe to publish as-is because it shows unauthenticated file reads and writes from request-controlled values and disables cache signatures.

high_findings.title: Unsafe Self-Hosted Cache Server Template
high_findings.description: The Express remote cache example builds file paths from request parameters and query values, then streams or writes cache artifacts without showing authentication, path validation, or size limits. Users who copy this template could expose arbitrary cache artifacts or allow untrusted writes within the cache directory.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly joins request-controlled team and hash values into filesystem paths and performs read and write streams. The context is a copyable server template, so the risk is real even though the skill file itself is documentation.

medium_findings.title: Remote Cache Signatures Disabled in Example
medium_findings.description: The self-hosted cache configuration sets remote cache signature verification to false. This can weaken artifact integrity if users adopt the example for shared or untrusted cache infrastructure.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sample explicitly disables signatures in a remote cache context. It may be acceptable for local testing, but it is risky guidance for production use.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Environment References Need Guardrails
medium_findings.description: The examples reference .env files and CI cache credentials. These references are legitimate Turborepo configuration patterns, but marketplace users should be warned not to cache secret values or expose tokens in generated configuration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file does not read or exfiltrate secrets, but it does teach users where to place cache credentials and environment dependencies. The risk is configuration misuse rather than malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Mostly Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Most external command alerts are Markdown fenced examples for Turborepo, npm, and CI usage. They are not executed by the skill, but they should still be reviewed before copying into production workflows.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command text appears inside Markdown examples and no automation runs it. The finding is retained as low risk because users may copy commands into their own repositories.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation and Localhost References
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to the official Turborepo schema, official documentation links, and a localhost cache example. No evidence found of data exfiltration to an unexpected external endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation or configuration examples and match the Turborepo topic. There is no hidden request logic in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary documentation words such as cache key concepts and request artifact hashes. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation or intentional weak algorithm use in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is descriptive Markdown or URL construction for cache artifacts. It does not implement MD5, SHA1, or another weak cryptographic primitive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Request-Controlled Filesystem Path Construction
dangerous_patterns.description: The self-hosted cache example combines CACHE_DIR with team and hash values from HTTP requests before reading and writing files. Production guidance should validate path segments and enforce authorization before filesystem access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem operations are directly tied to request-derived values in the displayed code. This is a concrete insecure pattern in a copyable example.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Cache Integrity Disabled
dangerous_patterns.description: The remoteCache sample disables signatures, which can reduce protection against tampered artifacts when using shared remote cache storage.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The configuration explicitly sets signature to false under remoteCache. The risk depends on deployment context but is clear enough for a warning.

content.value_statement: Turborepo caching is hard to configure across local builds, CI, and remote cache systems. This skill provides practical templates and review guidance for faster, more predictable monorepo builds.
actual_capabilities: Explains Turborepo pipeline concepts such as inputs, outputs, dependsOn, cache, and persistent tasks.
Provides turbo.json templates for root workspaces and package-specific overrides.
Shows CI examples for Turborepo builds, tests, filters, and remote cache variables.
Documents Turborepo filter patterns for packages, dependencies, dependents, and changed projects.
Lists debugging commands for dry runs, graph output, verbosity, forced rebuilds, and summaries.
Includes a self-hosted remote cache example that requires security hardening before production use.
limitations: It does not inspect a repository unless the assistant reads project files separately.
It does not run Turborepo commands automatically by itself.
The self-hosted cache server example is incomplete and needs authentication, validation, and integrity controls.
Version examples may need updates for current Turborepo releases and repository package managers.

use_cases.title: Create a Monorepo Cache Baseline
use_cases.description: Define root Turborepo tasks, cache outputs, dependencies, and environment inputs for a new monorepo.

use_cases.title: Speed Up CI Builds
use_cases.description: Add filters, remote cache variables, and build steps that run only affected packages in CI.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Cache Misses
use_cases.description: Use dry runs, summaries, graph output, and verbose logs to explain why tasks rerun.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a basic Turborepo caching setup for a monorepo with apps and packages. Explain the build, test, lint, and dev tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Tune CI Caching
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my CI workflow for Turborepo. Suggest cache outputs, affected-package filters, and remote cache variables that reduce build time.

prompt_templates.title: Debug Cache Misses
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through diagnosing Turborepo cache misses. Include commands to inspect hashes, task graphs, changed packages, and environment inputs.

prompt_templates.title: Design Secure Remote Cache
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a secure remote cache approach for Turborepo. Include authentication, artifact integrity, path validation, retention, and CI integration concerns.

output_examples.input: Set up Turborepo caching for a new workspace.
output_examples.output: A root configuration plan with build outputs, task dependencies, environment inputs, package scripts, and validation commands.

output_examples.input: Improve a slow CI pipeline that builds every package.
output_examples.output: A CI optimization checklist covering affected-package filters, remote cache credentials, dependency installation, and verification steps.

output_examples.input: Explain why a package is missing cache hits.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting sequence that checks task inputs, output paths, environment variables, dependency graphs, and forced rebuild settings.

best_practices: Define explicit inputs and outputs for tasks that should produce stable cache keys.
Use remote caching with protected tokens and clear team ownership in CI.
Disable caching for dev servers, deployments, database writes, and other side-effect-heavy tasks.
anti_patterns: Do not cache tasks that depend on hidden state or mutate external systems.
Do not place secret values in cacheable outputs or generated artifacts.
Do not deploy the self-hosted cache example without authentication and path validation.

faq.question: Does this skill configure Turborepo automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and templates that an assistant can adapt after reviewing your repository.

faq.question: Can it help with remote caching?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Vercel remote caching, CI variables, and self-hosted cache concepts.

faq.question: Is the self-hosted cache example production ready?
faq.answer: No. It needs authentication, path validation, artifact integrity checks, logging, and storage controls.

faq.question: Can it diagnose cache misses?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists commands and checks for inputs, outputs, environment variables, filters, and task graphs.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks the skill as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I provide before using it?
faq.answer: Share your package manager, workspace layout, current turbo.json, CI workflow, and the tasks you want to cache.
